title: Vivi Begs for Help
description: On pages 5–8, vivi begs for help as the Straw Hats reach Drum Island, but the armed villagers fear pirates and refuse to welcome them until Vivi pleads for Nami’s life.
scene: Vivi Begs for Help. The Straw Hats reach Drum Island, but the armed villagers fear pirates and refuse to welcome them until Vivi pleads for Nami’s life. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: More import- Andy...

dialogue.speaker: Drum villager
dialogue.text: Don't you think it's been pretty cold every day for awhile now?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Yeah I guess now that you mention it.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: It's probably just another bizarre aspect to the Grand Line...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No, it's proof that we're close to an island.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Sanji, watch carefully for land on the horizon.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: VIVI?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: There must be a "Winter Island" nearby.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Winter Island?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: aw!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: In terms of climate, the islands of grand line can be classified into 4 types.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Summer Island, Spring Island, Autumn Island, and Winter Island.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: And each of these 4 islands generally have 4 seasons of their own.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: In short,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: In order to survive sailing across Grand Line,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: You should be able to endure all 16 types of seasons,

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: From the summer of summer island all the way to the winter of winter island.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Of course, there still are exceptions and unknown climates.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I see... so the reason why grand line has such unpredictable! Climate,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Is due to being trapped between all these islands, each with their own distinct climate...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Exactly.

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: That's why climate stabilization signals the presence of an island nearby...!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: You're right...

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I can see land up ahead!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Land Ahoy!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Land!?

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Nami, did you hear that! It's an island! Now we can get you all better!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Just go outside and look...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Whoa- AAAA- AAA!

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: It's an island!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: It's so white!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: It must be snow! A snow island!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Luffy! Let me just say now that we can't afford to go on an adventure this time.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Once we get a doctor to treat Nami, we're leaving right away.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No good, he's already off in his own world...

dialogue.speaker: Dalton
dialogue.text: Snow is so fun...

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: Hold on a moment... is that island gonna be safe? If there's snow, couldn't there be snow monsters or something!?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: There might not even be any humans living on it!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Oh no! My can't-enter- island-itis is acting up again!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: I like snow 'cause it's white.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: get ready to dock, guys.

summary: This part centers on vivi begs for help, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
